name: Negusie v. Holder
question: Can a foreign citizen who is denied asylum due to his involvement in the persecution of others on the basis of a protected ground argue that he was compelled to do so by his military superiors and, on several occasions, actually attempted to help those who were being persecuted?
description: A case in which the Court held that the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA) incorrectly interpreted provisions of the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) from a mistaken legal premise.
